text:
The Core Issue: CR10 (Top 10 Concentration Rate) exceeds 48% This creates tangible risks:
- Voting power controlled by a minority
- Vulnerability to governance attacks
- Power entrenchment and corruption risks
- Barriers to new DRep entry
- Exclusion of diverse voices

I am sharing my Two-layered Solution:
1️⃣ Hard Cap at 87M (1.5% limit)
✅ Structurally limits single DRep influence
✅ Increases cost of multi-DRep splitting
✅ Expands opportunities for new/small DReps

2️⃣ Annual 1-2 Delegation Resets
✅ Breaks default delegation inertia
✅ Periodically raises governance awareness
✅ Reduces passive delegation
✅ Encourages conscious re-selection (Combined with staking reward lock)
✅ Reduces delegation on inactive DReps
✅ Provides entry opportunities for new DReps
While this creates some UX friction for users, requiring delegation review 1-2 times per year can increase governance engagement.
This would lead Cardano towards a healthier ecosystem.
#CardanoGovernance #Target15

"Governance Architecture and Enforcement
The governance architecture of a dOSPO serves a single objective: enable coordinated execution without allowing authority to consolidate. This requires explicit separation of powers, bounded mandates, and enforcement mechanisms that do not rely on trust or permanence.
A dOSPO operates through three policy domains, each governed by a distinct community-mandated body. These domains are intentionally general so that any sufficiently mature Web3 ecosystem can adapt them to its own governance structures."

text:
MCC Monthly Update - February 2026

February was a busy month for the Intersect Membership & Community Committee (MCC). Some of the main topics worked on included continued collaboration on the hubs initiative, updates regarding outreach, refinement of objectives for social media and education efforts, and work toward the finalization of the funding proposal for the new fiscal year.

Committee members and volunteers continued collaborating on the plan for the revitalization of hubs. Discussion took place throughout the month to ensure a robust plan is formulated for optimal success of the hub effort.

Other MCC objectives moved forward during the month of February included the completion and submission of materials for guidance in creating a standardized voice and structure for messaging representing Intersect across social media platforms.

Additionally, a new member welcome and orientation page was created and submitted for final approval.

Social media and education initiatives formed part of discussion at committee meetings throughout the month. Feedback from committee members helped to further fine-tune messaging and ensure social media posts align with the outreach and education mission of the MCC and of Intersect as a whole.

MCC Open Office X Spaces were held each Monday of the month. This allowed opportunities for the broader community to ask questions directly to MCC members and volunteers, gain clarity on concerns, and discuss topics on the MCC agenda.

Another significant subject of discussion and collaboration that continued in the month of February was work on finalization of the funding proposal.
Feedback was requested and discussion occurred regarding refining the final proposal, optimizing the suggested areas for funding to ensure the highest possible effectiveness in the projected use of funds for various areas under the purview of of the MCC,  and ensuring the proposed allocation of funds reflects the needs of the MCC throughout the upcoming fiscal year.

Check out the Intersect Discord channel for more updates, meeting times, and ways to get involved!

text:
Hi Intersect, thank you for your work on the Budget Process Framework.

I would like to clarify the definition of "participating stake" in the following passage:

"Items will be consolidated into the Treasury Withdrawal phase only if they meet the requirement of a yes vote of equal or greater than 67% of participating stake in the Ekklesia poll."

Which of the following definitions applies?

[1]. Stake of DReps who voted Yes/No on that specific proposal
[2]. Stake of DReps who voted Yes/No/Abstain on that specific proposal
[3]. Stake of DReps who voted Yes/No on any proposal in the round
[4]. Stake of DReps who voted Yes/No/Abstain on any proposal in the round

To illustrate the difference, consider the following scenario:

- DRep X (100M ADA): votes Yes on Proposal A, does not vote on Proposal B
- DRep Y (200M ADA): votes Abstain on Proposal A, does not vote on Proposal B
- DRep Z (300M ADA): does not vote on Proposal A, votes No on Proposal B
- DRep W (400M ADA): does not vote on Proposal A, votes Abstain on Proposal B

Under each definition, the participating stake denominator for Proposal A would be:

[1] X only (Yes on A) = 100M ADA
[2] X + Y (Yes/Abstain on A) = 300M ADA
[3] X + Z (Yes/No in the round, Abstain excluded) = 400M ADA
[4] X + Y + Z + W (any vote in the round) = 1000M ADA

text:
🚨🌐Cardano次期ハードフォークが目前へ──Node 10.7.0がエコシステム統合の起点に

Cardanoのアップグレード状況について、Intersectから最新の「Upgrade Bulletin #6」が公開されました。

今回のポイントは、次期ハードフォーク候補リリース（HFC）に向けた最終段階に入ってきたという点です。
まず現在の状況ですが、テストネットの SanchoNet はすでに新しいCardanoノードにアップグレードされており、Protocol Version 11 の機能テストが進められています。

また、Plutusの新しいコストモデルも準備が進んでおり、すでにSanchoNetではテスト可能な状態になっています。
現在公開されているノードは Cardano Node 10.6.2。

これは約3週間前にリリースされたもので、ハードフォークに関する機能をテストできるバージョンですが、メインネット用の正式なハードフォーク候補ではありません。
次に予定されているのが Cardano Node 10.7.0 です。

このバージョンは
・1週間以内のリリース予定
・メインネットハードフォーク候補（HFC）になる予定
・エコシステムツールとの統合ポイントになる
とされており、リリース後は

Preview → PreProd → Mainnet

という順番でネットワークがフォークされる見込みです。

ただし、パフォーマンス結果や統合テストの状況によっては、さらに小さなマイナーアップデートが追加される可能性もあります。

また今回のアップグレードは、単なるハードフォーク対応だけではなく、新機能も含む大きなノードアップデートになる予定です。

そのため、ウォレット、dApp、インフラ、ツールなど、エコシステム全体がNode 10.7への統合作業を開始することが重要とされています。

コミュニティ向けの動きとしては、今週から
・Hard Forkワーキンググループが毎週ミーティング
・毎週木曜日にXスペース開催（日本時間だと深夜）
など、アップグレードに向けた情報共有も強化されます。

Cardanoの大型アップグレードは、

ノード → テストネット → エコシステム統合 → メインネット

という段階的プロセスで進みます。

🚨🇨🇭スイスのスーパーでADA決済が現実に──Cardanoが「日常インフラ」に近づく大きな一歩

Cardano Foundationの発表によると、スイス国内のスーパー「SPAR」137店舗で、暗号資産ADAによる支払いが可能になりました。

これは、スイスの暗号決済インフラ企業https://t.co/8MhFTxeM1QとFinTech企業Brick Towersとの連携によって実現したものです。
基盤となっているのは、https://t.co/8MhFTxeM1Qが開発した暗号決済標準「Open Crypto Pay」です。

この仕組みによって、ユーザーはネイティブADAウォレットから直接、レジでADA支払いができるようになります。
しかも、中央集権型取引所を経由せず、リアルタイムで決済が処理されます。

小売業者にとってもメリットがあります。
従来のカード決済や決済プロバイダーと比較して、手数料をおよそ3分の1程度まで削減できる可能性があるとのことです。
これは、ブロックチェーン決済が単なる技術的実験ではなく、経済的にも意味を持つことを示しています。

さらに、https://t.co/8MhFTxeM1Qはオンランプ／オフランプの仕組みを提供しており、ADAと法定通貨の交換も直接行えます。
つまり、銀行システムとCardanoエコシステムの橋渡しをする役割も担っています。

加えて、Brick TowersのFinTechアプリ「urble」では、ADAを使った貯蓄機能も提供されます。
子どもや家族のための貯蓄目標を設定し、デジタル資産を長期的な資産形成に活用できる仕組みです。

Cardano FoundationのCEO、フレデリック・グレガード氏は次のように述べています。

「ブロックチェーンが“見えないインフラ”になり、ADAで支払うことがカードと同じくらい自然になったとき、金融の本当の変革が始まる」

今回の取り組みは、その未来に向けた一つの重要なステップと言えそうです。

text:
エポックな日々：616

世界秩序の転換点──戦争・AI・暗号資産が作る次の金融システム
🔽
https://t.co/MWqpjAivV3

エポックな日々616を公開しました。

今回の記事では、

・中東情勢やウクライナ戦争などの地政学
・米国の暗号資産政策（GENIUS Act / Clarity Act）
・CBDCを2030年まで止めるという動き
・KrakenがFRB決済ネットワークに直結したニュース
・ウォール街やCrypto企業の銀行化
・X Moneyが描く「金融OS」
・AIエージェントによるマシン経済

こうした一見バラバラに見える出来事を、

「金融インフラの再設計」

という視点で整理してみました。

最近のニュースを見ていると、暗号資産は単なる投資市場というより、

金融インフラの議論の中心

に入り始めているようにも見えます。

例えば、

KrakenがFRBのマスターアカウントを取得し、中央銀行の決済レイヤーに直接接続しました。

ウォール街ではビットコインが金融資産として扱われ始めています。

さらにCrypto企業が銀行ライセンスを取得する流れも出てきました。

そしてイーロン・マスクのXは、SNSを超えて「金融OS」を作ろうとしている可能性があります。

つまり今起きているのは

Crypto vs 金融

ではなく、

Crypto × 金融

という融合のプロセスなのかもしれません。

さらにここに加わるのがAIです。

AIエージェントがウォレットを持ち、支払いを行い、経済活動を行う世界。

いわゆる「マシン経済」です。

もしこの世界が実現するとすれば、AIにとって使いやすい金融インフラは

銀行口座ではなく
ブロックチェーン

になる可能性があります。

そしてこの文脈の中で、CardanoとMidnightの役割も少し整理してみました。

Cardanoは金融インフラとしての制度設計を重視するブロックチェーン。

Midnightは、規制とプライバシーのバランスを取る「選択的プライバシー」を目指すネットワークです。

もしブロックチェーンが本当に金融インフラになるのであれば、

・安全性
・持続性
・ガバナンス
・プライバシー

といった要素は避けて通れません。

その意味で、今の暗号資産の世界は単なる投資市場ではなく、

次の金融秩序の実験場

とも言えるのではないでしょうか。

地政学
国家政策
金融インフラ
AI

これらが同時に動いている今、

世界はもしかすると

金融再起動の時代

に入りつつあるのかもしれません。

よろしければぜひ記事をご覧ください。

🔧 Protocol Version 11 Hard Fork steht bevor! 🚀

Cardano bereitet ein wichtiges Intra-Era-Upgrade vor, das die Performance massiv verbessern wird:

✅ Plutus Smart Contracts schneller & günstiger
✅ Neue kryptografische Funktionen
✅ Verbesserte Netzwerk-Sicherheit
✅ Node 10.6.2 bereits released

Das Upgrade ist Non-Breaking - heißt: Keine Störungen für User oder dApps. Das ist die Vorbereitung auf das große Leios-Upgrade, das Cardano auf 1.000+ TPS bringen soll.

#Cardano #ADA #HardFork #ProtocolUpgrade #Plutus #SmartContracts

Ein Hard Fork ist ein größeres Netzwerk-Upgrade. 'Intra-Era' bedeutet, dass es innerhalb der aktuellen Entwicklungsphase (Basho) stattfindet, ohne die gesamte Architektur zu ändern. Plutus ist die Smart-Contract-Sprache von Cardano - schnellere Contracts bedeuten günstigere Transaktionen und komplexere dApps werden möglich. Die neuen kryptografischen Funktionen bereiten das Netzwerk auch auf zukünftige Bedrohungen (z.B. Quantum Computing) vor. Für Staker ändert sich nichts - deine ADA sind sicher. Aber das Ökosystem wird attraktiver für Entwickler, was langfristig mehr Aktivität und Wert ins Netzwerk bringen kann.

For the given use-case though you don’t need merkle trees for “any of 50”, you could just store the wishlist as a simple list of bytearrays in the utxo, you’d be able to store between 350-500 in a single snipe utxo, and the execution costs would be cheaper because proofs become lookups. text: It’s indeterministic for sure, but it’s often the case that if a run starts in the right direction it ends with the correct answer and if it starts in the wrong direction it has no chance of success (accuracy over longer running tasks decrease, and because llms are predictive models, the further from the correct answer their current prediction is the further subsequent predictions will be, this is why accuracy spikes if you copy paste your prompt twice, or you add your prompt directly in the body of the relevant function so that every time it looks at the code it gets your prompt in context again.

author: IntersectMBO

text:
ADA holders are responsible for the quality of Cardano’s decentralization.

The problem is that they don’t care much about decentralization.

This was proven in the past in the debates about the MPO problem. Now the problem is repeated in governance.

The situation is made worse because stakers are forced to delegate to DRep without any incentivization for the quality of their choice.

While in staking, the choice of SPO directly affects rewards, in governance, nothing like that applies. 

Most stakers simply abstain or delegate to Wallet DRep.

Credit goes to the 4% of stakers who chose other active DReps.

Let’s look at the decentralization of the network.

In Cardano, we have ~2,400 registered pools. That sounds cool, right?

Of these, ~900 pools have such a low stake that they do not mine any blocks. They are basically dead.

About ~600 pools have a stake of up to 1M, so they occasionally mine a block. They are struggling to survive. We have seen many of these pools retire recently.

So only ~900 are doing well. Many of them are multi-pool operators.

The top 10 operators together run 253 pools and a large amount of the stake. 

That doesn’t look so cool anymore.

We have ~2400 pools, but only 900 are profitable. 

1500 pools are struggling to survive. 

They would like to produce blocks, but the system doesn’t allow them to, and stakers are not willing to change delegations.

Let's help small SPOs before they retire and never come back. Maybe this is the last chance to do it.

Do you see the parallels with governance? 

We have 1000 registered DReps, but only ~250 vote.

Other DReps might like to vote, but their voting power is so low that it doesn't make sense considering the time and effort.

Top 5 DReps can block Treasury Withdrawals and Constitutional amendments if they collude.

What's the solution?

In the case of the network, it's easy. We have a parameter k that the community can change via an on-chain proposal. A draft proposal even exists.

If k increases to 1000, new motivated SPOs will emerge from the 1500 pools that are trying to survive. 

This could have a positive social aspect. While OG MPOs/SPOs may be tired or burned out, this would be fresh blood for the ecosystem.

All we need to do is find consensus in the community and submit a proposal. It can be solved within a month. 

With governance, the change will be more difficult because there are no incentives, except for the negative one that forces stakers to delegate to DRep (when they want to withdraw staking rewards).

There is no consensus in the community on how many DReps we need. We even want to (perhaps illogically) increase the number of DReps without first asking why 70% of registered DReps do not vote.

We do not know how to motivate stakers to make a deliberate choice of DRep. Forcing stakers to redelegate after a certain period may not work without other positive incentives. 

As we already know, they will not bother with governance.

We do not know how to motivate DReps to vote. It seems that even those in the top 100 are exhausted. 

Nearly all proposals are decided in the last epochs. The community must alert DReps to vote. Instead, proposals could be approved in an epoch or two.

Introducing a saturation mechanism in governance or recalculating voting power seems to be the only logical way. Implementation in the protocol may take months, maybe a year.

Meanwhile, we have a vote on important spending ahead of us.

We are not able to address this quickly. In my view, it is necessary to link staking with governance. Stakers must be economically incentivized not only to pick a great SPO, but also pick a favorite DRep.

Maybe it makes sense to use staking power in governance. If SPO wanted to, it could become a DRep with the power of one of its pools.

We probably need a complete redesign of incentives. We also need to address decreasing rewards.

Anyway, increasing the K parameter to 1000 could be a first step toward tracking changes in the system.

If decentralization improves, it would be a good sign for governance.
author: JaromirTesar

text:
@NaVi_GaT0R @IntersectMBO This is exactly the kind of situation that shows why Cardano struggles to govern itself.

You just described months of coordination with Pentad, Intersect, the Cardano Foundation, Fireblocks, and ecosystem teams.

And after all of that alignment and work, the team actually building the solution ends up eating the cost.

That is the kind of incentive structure that makes serious builders think twice about working in this ecosystem. No competent team walks into an environment where doing the work that benefits everyone most likely ends with them taking a loss.

Respect for taking it on the chin and still delivering the integration. But situations like this are exactly why people hesitate to build here in the first place.

text:
One cool thing about strategies:

This isn't public signal; nobody sees on-chain *which* strategy you're using. It could be a DCA, a trailing stop loss, something that someone wrote custom for themselves, a button that a human is pressing to trigger the trades, or a decaying cesium atom that trades randomly. Nobody gets to see the configuration either.

There is no on-chain data that let's someone identify "if I drop the price by 1%, it will trigger this much sell-off from the trialing stop losses, and at 5% it'll trigger this much".

1. Delegation IS linked to -withdrawing- staking rewards, meaning for users who don't care but just want their staking rewards, they delegate to the first DRep presented to them to "check the box" and get their bacon
2. Yoroi + Eternl DReps are so powerful because they are the two most popular wallets and both have default interfaces that make it "easy" to simply delegate to their DRep.

One can argue that there is a certain amount of personal responsibility that should be involved, but the design of the system (forcing a delegation in order to claim rewards) has lent itself to the passive majority (not on Twitter, not using dApps, etc) simply checking the box and moving on with their life, still blissfully unaware of governance activity or responsibility

text:
"The Problem: Open Source as Web3 Infrastructure
What a Web3 Ecosystem Looks Like

A Web3 ecosystem is a network of independent, loosely coordinated entities that collectively build and operate decentralized infrastructure. Unlike traditional software organizations with clear hierarchies, a mature Web3 ecosystem typically includes protocol research and engineering organizations responsible for core protocol design and node software, foundations focused on stewardship, education, and regulatory engagement, commercial entities driving enterprise adoption and tooling, community governance bodies such as DAOs, committees, and councils that direct treasury funds and set policy, and independent developers and validators who build applications and operate infrastructure.

Each of these entities is legitimate within its scope. None has unilateral authority over the whole. All of them depend on the same shared open source infrastructure: protocol clients, cryptographic libraries, wallets, SDKs, testing frameworks, CI systems, and monitoring tools. This infrastructure functions as a public good, nonexcludable and essential to every participant, yet no single entity is formally responsible for its sustained maintenance.

The presence of a native token profoundly shapes this landscape. Tokens create economic incentives, fund treasuries, and enable governance participation, but they also introduce dynamics absent from traditional open source: speculative pressure on funding decisions, token-weighted voting that can concentrate influence, and misaligned incentives between short-term token value and long-term infrastructure health."

Read full paper here: https://t.co/P5h0PJmcnY

text:
After around 4 months of back and forth with Pentad & Intersect. Pentad steering committee decided not to reimburse Iagon for the Fireblocks integration fee. 

@IntersectMBO recommend it to be approved but Pentad committee decided against. 

This integration is a necessary stop-gap solution in order to provide institutional custody solutions for Cardano. If it goes well, this will lead to native integration. This is the solution that was recommend by Fireblocks to show ARR. 

We also made steps early on to contact a host of dApps, market makers, capital funds, that could benefit from this solution to see what type of features they would like to see within the integration. 

We even invited CF's Global Head of Technical Integrations @michaeldotada  to make sure the features were in alignment with the goals. Michiel also attended every meeting leading up to the commercial agreement with Fireblocks.

This is Cardano wide integration, helps every CNT and at the same time provides $ADA native staking and voting features. 

It is worth noting that FB wanted to work with us on this stop-gap integration and not the pentad members. They had the option of working with others and choose not to. Prior to pentad formalizing, we were assured by many of their members that they would have no objection to this reimbursement. Our plan was to go to the treasury prior to their pentad proposal being proposed within the Cardano treasury. We waited because of these verbal assurances. 

@IagonOfficial will still continue this integration for Cardano and pay for the full fee. We don't plan to go to the treasury for a refund. We hope you like what we deliver. Excited for the end result!

text:
We need to get some liquidity incentive proposals passed from the treasury. 30-80M treasury withdrawal that is used to pay APY incentives to USDCx in DeFi will generate much more stablecoin issuance / TVL than simply selling the equivalent withdrawn amount for USD and using that to mint USDCx and manage the portfolio. 

Liquidity is sticky, and the best way to get it is to subsidize incentives for it. To get 100M USDCx on Cardano for a year we would only need to pay roughly $6M USD max, in practice probably much less ($2-4M). To get 100M ada TVL increase for a year, it would cost roughly 2-6% of that as-well. This is the meta that all other DeFi ecosystems use for boosting TVL, promotional incentives, ex. AAVE.

text:
Swaps on Cardano should leverage the full feature set of the eUTXO model: deterministic, parallelizable, and composable.

Gravity is a batcherless DEX that allows swaps to settle as a single transaction rather than through a coordinated pipeline.

That design simplifies fees, makes swaps more reliable when things get busy, and you handle the execution (not some middleman).

Cardano never had its Uniswap moment, and we want to give it a try.

text:
"The Open Source Governance Landscape: Four Models and Their Limits
1) DAO-Only 
2) Foundations
3) Corporate OSPO's
4) d(OSPO)'s

The following framework evaluates each model along three core dimensions: legitimacy (where authority comes from and whether it is trusted), execution capacity (whether the model can act decisively under time pressure), and neutrality (whether the model resists capture by any single interest). These dimensions define a governance triangle within which every model must find its position. The dOSPO is introduced here as the fourth operational model, completing the landscape."

Read the full analysis here: https://t.co/P5h0PJmcnY

At MWC 2026, the World Mobile Stratospheric team has been presenting our high-altitude connectivity platform to governments, telecom operators, and infrastructure partners from around the world.
 
The interest has been huge.
 
A hydrogen-powered aircraft carrying a phased-array 5G antenna, using AI-driven beamforming to deliver connectivity direct to handsets from 20,000 meters.
 
No towers.
No terrain limits.
Just global coverage.
 
Here’s a glimpse of what we’re showcasing this week.
 
The future of connectivity is taking flight.
